title: phragmites australis var. marsillianus
intro: Phragmites australis var. marsillianus, also known as sweet grass or wild celery, is a variant of the phragmites australis plant, a type of grass that is native to North America. It has been used by Native Americans for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of phragmites australis var. marsillianus include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and promote a good night's sleep. Herbal preparations of phragmites australis var. marsillianus can be made into teas, infusions, or smoked as a traditional medicine, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
